description: Add per-tool icon support for built-in browser tools and replace the browser tools card header icon with the Chrome logo SVG. Browser tools currently hardcode a globe icon for every tool — this adds an icon field to BrowserToolDefinition, threads it through the wire protocol, and renders per-tool icons in the side panel.

userStories.title: Add icon field to BrowserToolDefinition and wire types
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want browser tools to declare a Lucide icon name so that each tool can be visually distinguished in the side panel.
acceptanceCriteria: BrowserToolDefinition in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/definition.ts has an optional icon field typed as string
ConfigStateBrowserTool in platform/shared/src/index.ts has an optional icon field typed as string
CachedBrowserTool in platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts includes the icon field
rebuildCachedBrowserTools in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts propagates the icon from BrowserToolDefinition to CachedBrowserTool
handleConfigGetState in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts includes the icon field when serializing browser tools
BrowserToolState in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts includes the icon field (inherited from ConfigStateBrowserTool)
userStories.notes:
This story adds the icon field to the data model layer without changing any browser tool definitions or UI rendering.

Files to modify:

1. platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/definition.ts — Add optional `icon?: string` field to the `BrowserToolDefinition` interface. Add a JSDoc comment: `/** Lucide icon name (kebab-case) displayed in the side panel. Defaults to 'globe' if omitted. */`. The icon field is a plain string, NOT the LucideIconName type from the plugin-sdk (the mcp-server package does not depend on plugin-sdk).

2. platform/shared/src/index.ts — Add optional `icon?: string` field to the `ConfigStateBrowserTool` interface (around line 292). Add JSDoc: `/** Lucide icon name (kebab-case) for the side panel */`.

3. platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts — Add optional `icon?: string` field to the `CachedBrowserTool` interface (around line 146).

4. platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts — In `rebuildCachedBrowserTools` (line 102-113), propagate the icon: add `...(bt.icon ? { icon: bt.icon } : {})` to the returned CachedBrowserTool object.

5. platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts — In `handleConfigGetState` (line 292-299), add `...(ct.icon ? { icon: ct.icon } : {})` to the mapped browser tool objects.

The bridge.ts file in the extension doesn't need changes — BrowserToolState is already typed as ConfigStateBrowserTool, so it will automatically pick up the new optional icon field.

Keep all new fields optional so existing browser tools without icons still work (they'll fall back to 'globe' in the UI, which is the current behavior).

userStories.title: Assign Lucide icons to all 37 browser tool definitions
userStories.description: As a user, I want each browser tool to have a distinct icon so that I can quickly identify tools visually in the side panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Every browser tool definition in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/*.ts has an icon field set to a valid Lucide icon name
Icon names are semantically appropriate for each tool's function
All icon names are valid kebab-case Lucide icon names
userStories.notes:
Add an `icon` field to every `defineBrowserTool({...})` call. Here is the complete mapping of tool name → icon:

Tab management:
- browser_list_tabs (list-tabs.ts): icon: 'layout-list'
- browser_open_tab (open-tab.ts): icon: 'plus'
- browser_close_tab (close-tab.ts): icon: 'x'
- browser_navigate_tab (navigate-tab.ts): icon: 'compass'
- browser_focus_tab (focus-tab.ts): icon: 'eye'
- browser_get_tab_info (get-tab-info.ts): icon: 'info'

Page interaction:
- browser_click_element (click-element.ts): icon: 'mouse-pointer-click'
- browser_type_text (type-text.ts): icon: 'keyboard'
- browser_select_option (select-option.ts): icon: 'chevrons-up-down'
- browser_hover_element (hover-element.ts): icon: 'hand'
- browser_press_key (press-key.ts): icon: 'command'
- browser_scroll (scroll.ts): icon: 'arrow-down-up'
- browser_handle_dialog (handle-dialog.ts): icon: 'message-square'

Inspection:
- browser_get_tab_content (get-tab-content.ts): icon: 'file-text'
- browser_get_page_html (get-page-html.ts): icon: 'code'
- browser_screenshot_tab (screenshot-tab.ts): icon: 'camera'
- browser_query_elements (query-elements.ts): icon: 'search'
- browser_wait_for_element (wait-for-element.ts): icon: 'clock'

Storage & cookies:
- browser_get_storage (get-storage.ts): icon: 'database'
- browser_get_cookies (get-cookies.ts): icon: 'cookie'
- browser_set_cookie (set-cookie.ts): icon: 'cookie'
- browser_delete_cookies (delete-cookies.ts): icon: 'trash-2'

Network:
- browser_enable_network_capture (enable-network-capture.ts): icon: 'radio'
- browser_get_network_requests (get-network-requests.ts): icon: 'activity'
- browser_get_websocket_frames (get-websocket-frames.ts): icon: 'cable'
- browser_export_har (export-har.ts): icon: 'download'
- browser_disable_network_capture (disable-network-capture.ts): icon: 'wifi-off'

Console:
- browser_get_console_logs (get-console-logs.ts): icon: 'terminal'
- browser_clear_console_logs (clear-console-logs.ts): icon: 'eraser'

Resources:
- browser_list_resources (list-resources.ts): icon: 'folder-tree'
- browser_get_resource_content (get-resource-content.ts): icon: 'file-code'

Extension diagnostics:
- extension_reload (reload-extension.ts): icon: 'rotate-cw'
- extension_get_state (extension-get-state.ts): icon: 'settings'
- extension_get_logs (extension-get-logs.ts): icon: 'scroll-text'
- extension_get_side_panel (extension-get-side-panel.ts): icon: 'panel-right'
- extension_check_adapter (extension-check-adapter.ts): icon: 'plug'
- extension_force_reconnect (extension-force-reconnect.ts): icon: 'refresh-cw'

Plugin tools:
- plugin_analyze_site (analyze-site.ts): icon: 'scan-search'
- plugin_list_tabs (plugin-list-tabs.ts): icon: 'list'

For each file, add the icon field after the description field. Example for click-element.ts:
```typescript
const clickElement = defineBrowserTool({
  name: 'browser_click_element',
  description: '...',
  icon: 'mouse-pointer-click',
  input: z.object({...}),
  handler: async (args, state) => ...,
});
```

All these icon names have been verified to exist in the lucide-react icon set. Do NOT skip any tool — all 37 must have icons.

userStories.title: Render per-tool icons in the browser tools side panel card
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to display each browser tool's unique icon instead of a generic globe icon.
acceptanceCriteria: BrowserToolsCard.tsx passes tool.icon (falling back to 'globe') to ToolRow instead of hardcoded 'globe'
The BrowserToolsCard header icon uses a Chrome logo SVG instead of the Lucide Globe icon
The Chrome SVG is stored in a dedicated file at platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ChromeIcon.tsx as a React component
The Chrome SVG is the official Google Chrome icon (February 2022) from Wikimedia Commons
userStories.notes:
Two changes in this story:

### 1. Per-tool icons in BrowserToolsCard.tsx

In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.tsx, line 135, change:
```tsx
icon="globe"
```
to:
```tsx
icon={tool.icon ?? 'globe'}
```

The `tool` object is typed as `BrowserToolState` which now has an optional `icon` field (from US-001). The fallback to 'globe' preserves backward compatibility.

Also update the `BrowserToolState` type import if needed — it comes from `../bridge.js` which re-exports `ConfigStateBrowserTool` from `@opentabs-dev/shared`, which now has the `icon` field.

### 2. Chrome logo SVG for the card header

Create a new file `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ChromeIcon.tsx` with a React component that renders the Chrome logo SVG inline. The SVG content is:

```tsx
const ChromeIcon = ({ className = '' }: { className?: string }) => (
  <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 48 48" className={className}>
    <defs>
      <linearGradient id="chrome-a" x1="3.2173" y1="15" x2="44.7812" y2="15" gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse">
        <stop offset="0" stopColor="#d93025" />
        <stop offset="1" stopColor="#ea4335" />
      </linearGradient>
      <linearGradient id="chrome-b" x1="20.7219" y1="47.6791" x2="41.5039" y2="11.6837" gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse">
        <stop offset="0" stopColor="#fcc934" />
        <stop offset="1" stopColor="#fbbc04" />
      </linearGradient>
      <linearGradient id="chrome-c" x1="26.5981" y1="46.5015" x2="5.8161" y2="10.506" gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse">
        <stop offset="0" stopColor="#1e8e3e" />
        <stop offset="1" stopColor="#34a853" />
      </linearGradient>
    </defs>
    <circle cx="24" cy="23.9947" r="12" fill="#fff" />
    <path d="M3.2154,36A24,24,0,1,0,12,3.2154,24,24,0,0,0,3.2154,36ZM34.3923,18A12,12,0,1,1,18,13.6077,12,12,0,0,1,34.3923,18Z" fill="none" />
    <path d="M24,12H44.7812a23.9939,23.9939,0,0,0-41.5639.0029L13.6079,30l.0093-.0024A11.9852,11.9852,0,0,1,24,12Z" fill="url(#chrome-a)" />
    <circle cx="24" cy="24" r="9.5" fill="#1a73e8" />
    <path d="M34.3913,30.0029,24.0007,48A23.994,23.994,0,0,0,44.78,12.0031H23.9989l-.0025.0093A11.985,11.985,0,0,1,34.3913,30.0029Z" fill="url(#chrome-b)" />
    <path d="M13.6086,30.0031,3.218,12.006A23.994,23.994,0,0,0,24.0025,48L34.3931,30.0029l-.0067-.0068a11.9852,11.9852,0,0,1-20.7778.007Z" fill="url(#chrome-c)" />
  </svg>
);

export { ChromeIcon };
```

IMPORTANT: The SVG uses `style` attributes in the original — convert them to JSX props. Specifically:
- `style="fill:#fff"` → `fill="#fff"`
- `style="fill:none"` → `fill="none"`
- `style="fill:url(#a)"` → `fill="url(#chrome-a)"`
- `stop-color` → `stopColor` (JSX camelCase)
- Prefix the gradient IDs with `chrome-` to avoid collisions with other SVGs on the page (e.g., `id="a"` → `id="chrome-a"`, and update all `url(#a)` references accordingly)

Then in BrowserToolsCard.tsx:
1. Remove the `Globe` import from lucide-react (line 10)
2. Add `import { ChromeIcon } from './ChromeIcon.js';`
3. Replace the Globe icon in the header (line 95):
   From: `<Globe className="text-muted-foreground h-4 w-4" />`
   To: `<ChromeIcon className="h-4 w-4" />`

Note: The ChromeIcon does NOT use `text-muted-foreground` because the Chrome logo has its own colors (red, yellow, green, blue). It only needs sizing classes.

If the `Globe` import is still used elsewhere in the file (check!), keep it. If not, remove it. Currently it's only used on line 95, so it can be removed.

Also check if knip flags the removed Globe import — it should be fine since we're removing the import along with its usage.
